verdict.answer_first: A; sentence 1 directly defines the point: "Big-O describes how an algorithm's cost grows as its input grows," while B starts with a title before the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: A; it commits to one practical prescription: "nested loop to set/dict lookup — is the single most common real-world Big-O optimization," while B ends with two patterns plus a follow-up menu.
verdict.fabrication: B has more unverifiable confidence: "Those two fixes cover the majority" and "fib(50) will hang your machine" are not evidenced. A is mostly framed as examples, though "freezes on 50,000 in production" is also illustrative, not proven.
verdict.actionability: A; it gives the wall, principle, and avoidance path in one clean chain: O(n²) duplicate check → "trade time for memory with a hash set" → O(n).
